label: Workflow, cluster & job orchestration

node.label: Workflow, cluster & job orchestration
node.definition: Workflow and cluster orchestration allocate resources, schedule jobs, express dependencies, retry failures, and coordinate multi-stage ML pipelines.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Workflow, cluster & job orchestration is to connect Distributed systems & networking, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations to downstream capabilities including Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps, Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence, Simulation farms, digital environments & scenario generation.
examples: substrate for Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps
substrate for Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence
substrate for Simulation farms, digital environments & scenario generation
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.

dependencies.rationale: Workflow, cluster & job orchestration turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Workflow, cluster & job orchestration turns Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Workflow, cluster & job orchestration turns Object storage, sharding & checkpoints into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Workflow, cluster & job orchestration turns Distributed data pipelines & streaming into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
